questions.prompt: Your webhook handler started returning signature verification failures after a framework upgrade. What's the most likely cause?

choices.text: Stripe rotated your secret without telling you.

Middleware is parsing JSON before your handler, modifying the raw bytes. Stripe's signature is HMAC over the raw body — any reformatting (parse + restringify, whitespace changes) breaks verification. Fix: preserve raw body on the webhook route. In Express: `express.raw({ type: 'application/json' })`. In Next.js App Router: `request.text()`.

questions.explanation: Raw-body bug is the #1 cause of mysterious signature failures. Frameworks often auto-parse JSON — that breaks signing. Preserve raw bytes on the webhook route specifically (not site-wide; other routes still want parsed JSON).

questions.prompt: Why is `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent` the right way to verify webhooks?

choices.text: It's the only API that exists.

It does HMAC signature verification with constant-time comparison (resistant to timing attacks), validates the timestamp (replay protection — rejects events older than 5 min by default), and throws on any failure. Rolling your own verification is error-prone; use the SDK helper on every webhook handler.

questions.explanation: `constructEvent` handles HMAC + constant-time compare + replay protection in one call. Rolling your own often has subtle bugs (string-vs-buffer comparison, missing replay check, timing leaks). Use the SDK.
